events.title: Wildfires in Ontario make Toronto air quality worst in world
events.description: Environment Canada has issued health warnings after sky over city turns yellow Smoke from more than 100 active wildfires in northern Ontario have made Toronto’s air quality the current worst in the world and caused yellow, smoky air in cities across the north-east US. Environment Canada issued health warnings on Wednesday after the sky over country’s largest city turned a sickly yellow and was ranked the worst in the world according to IQAir , the Swiss technology company that racks global air quality. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wildfires in Ontario make Toronto air quality worst in world

events.title: How global heating supercharged floods in West Africa, displacing thousands
events.description: Adaptation to frightening new normal and reducing emissions further and faster is critical, scientists warn Dozens of people drowned, hundreds had to be rescued and thousands were displaced when floods struck the coasts of west Africa last month. Now scientists have concluded that the rains that caused the floods were supercharged by climate breakdown. Global heating, they say, turned what should have been a routine weather event into a climate catastrophe. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ How global heating supercharged floods in West Africa, displacing thousands

events.title: First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC
events.description: Two drugs are being trialled in the Ituri region in a programme set up just six weeks after the outbreak was declared, with hopes it will reduce mortality rates There is no approved drug to help the medical teams scrabbling to save lives in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo – but there are hopes that could change within months as the first patients are enrolled in a treatment trial. It is a record pace to set up and start this kind of research, scientists said, with patients enrolled just six weeks after the outbreak being declared a public health emergency of international concern by the World Health Organization (WHO) on 17 May. Continue reading...

events.title: Bangkok bar fire: death toll reaches 30 as police say negligence is ‘primary theory’
events.description: Bar owner offers ‘deepest apologies’ as police investigate whether exits were either blocked or hard to access The Bangkok pub that became the scene of the city’s deadliest blaze in 17 years has said it will cooperate with an investigation into alleged negligence, as the death toll rose to 30. The local district office said on Tuesday that three more people had died after the devastating fire that broke out in the early hours of Monday. An initial assessment by disaster officials found an electrical short ‌circuit in an air conditioner located in the ‌ceiling had caused the fire. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bangkok bar fire: death toll reaches 30 as police say negligence is ‘primary theory’

events.title: New Pegasus spyware revelations as French PM visits Morocco
events.description: PRESS REVIEW - Thursday, July 7: we look into new revelations on the Moroccan surveillance apparatus, five years after the first wave of the Pegasus spyware scandal. In other news, support for Israel is floundering on both sides of the political spectrum in Washington ahead of Israeli prime Minister Bejamin Netanyahu's visit this weekend. Finally, we look at international reactions to the World Cup semi-final, when Argentina made a historic comeback, quashing the Three Lions' hope of putting an end to 60 years of hurt.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New Pegasus spyware revelations as French PM visits Morocco

events.title: Gunfire shatters Toronto Latin street festival, leaving at least 2 dead and multiple wounded
events.description: At least two people were killed and four others wounded after gunfire erupted at Toronto's TD Salsa on St. Clair Festival. Police are investigating the deadly shooting.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Gunfire shatters Toronto Latin street festival, leaving at least 2 dead and multiple wounded

events.title: Confirmed Ebola cases top 2,000 in Congo, including 754 deaths
events.description: Ebola cases in Congo have reached 2,011, with 754 deaths, according to government data

events.title: ‘Cycle of escalation must end’: UN condemns deadly Strait of Hormuz attacks
events.description: The UN maritime agency, IMO, condemned overnight attacks on shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz that killed at least two seafarers as fresh strikes were reported early Tuesday in the escalating US-Iran war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Cycle of escalation must end’: UN condemns deadly Strait of Hormuz attacks

events.title: Iran targets military bases as US launches wave of strikes
events.description: Explosions were heard across Iran overnight, shortly after neighbouring Gulf states began to report attacks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran targets military bases as US launches wave of strikes

events.title: ‘Malvinas are Argentinian’: World Cup holders celebrate win over England with Falklands banner
events.description: Banner refers to Falkland Islands conflict in 1982 UK business secretary calls for Fifa investigation The Argentina players celebrated their World Cup win over England with a banner saying “ Las Malvinas son Argentinas ”, making reference to the 1982 Falklands war . Argentina were 1-0 down with five minutes to go of the semi-final in Atlanta but rallied and scored twice in quick succession to reach a second straight World Cup final , where they will face Spain in New Jersey on Sunday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Malvinas are Argentinian’: World Cup holders celebrate win over England with Falklands banner

events.title: Iran threatens regional strikes as US intensifies attacks
events.description: Iran has struck sites in Jordan and Gulf states after the US military widened its set of Iranian targets, including in the north of the country. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran threatens regional strikes as US intensifies attacks

events.title: World News in Brief: Aid deliveries to Gaza restricted, UN prepares El Nino response, El Salvador eliminates disease
events.description: The UN World Food Programme (WFP) is calling for greater humanitarian access to Gaza, warning that restrictions on aid deliveries, ongoing violence and funding shortages are severely limiting its ability to reach people in need.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Aid deliveries to Gaza restricted, UN prepares El Nino response, El Salvador eliminates disease

events.title: Louisiana man accused of killing deputy US marshal faces possible death penalty
events.description: Authorities said Deputy U.S. Marshal Drew Hanson was fatally shot after Clarence A. Frazier Jr. allegedly barricaded himself inside his home and opened fire while officers served an arrest warrant.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Louisiana man accused of killing deputy US marshal faces possible death penalty

events.title: Iran-backed terror proxy Houthis threaten fresh attacks after Yemen airport strike
events.description: The Houthis threatened to strike King Khalid Airport in Riyadh after what they called "blatant aggression" targeting the Sanaa airport runway.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran-backed terror proxy Houthis threaten fresh attacks after Yemen airport strike

events.title: Trump administration probes UNRWA over alleged employment of 1,500 terrorists
events.description: A senior U.S. official says the USAID OIG investigation picked up where the U.N. failed, examining UNRWA staff links to terrorism beyond Oct. 7.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Trump administration probes UNRWA over alleged employment of 1,500 terrorists

events.title: Killings continue on Del Monte farm in Kenya, families say, after G4S hired for security
events.description: Exclusive: Three men killed in incidents over past year allegedly involving G4S guards, who replaced in-house team after previous deaths Bereaved families and politicians have raised alarm about continued killings on Del Monte’s pineapple farm in Kenya despite the company hiring a British security firm to replace its in-house security team after previous deaths were exposed by the Guardian. The multinational food company appointed G4S to guard the farm, which is estimated to cover at least 40 sq km, the area of a small city, after the Guardian detailed allegations of brutal assaults and killings of people suspected of trespassing on its land. Kenyan police have been working with G4S to guard the site. Continue reading...

events.title: Algerian farmers on the frontline of heatwave-driven wildfires
events.description: Wildfires are raging across Algeria, where extreme heat and prolonged drought have fuelled dozens of blazes. Authorities say more than 130 fires were reported on July 15 alone. At least one person has been killed, and the damage has been devastating— especially for farmers and livestock owners. Wildfires are common in Algeria during the summer, but they have been unusually severe this year.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Algerian farmers on the frontline of heatwave-driven wildfires

events.title: Toronto suffocates as wildfire smoke and heatwave choke city
events.description: In Canada, more than 100 active wildfires continue to burn in northern Ontario. The smoke has reached Toronto, where authorities are urging residents to stay indoors as air quality has dropped. The city's air quality ranked the worst in the world on July 15 as its skies were shrouded in smoke, bringing allergic reactions to many.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Toronto suffocates as wildfire smoke and heatwave choke city

events.title: Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to full-on war
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres voiced deep concern on Wednesday over the continuing military escalation in the Middle East amid ongoing strikes by the United States and Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to full-on war

events.title: Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur
events.description: On Wednesday, the Security Council heard an update on the International Criminal Court's work in Sudan's Darfur region, referred to the Court back in 2005. Focus has recently turned to atrocities in the ongoing war between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). The briefing was held as the ICC faces mounting pressure: this week, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio launched a campaign to “dismantle” the Court, which prosecutes individuals for genocide, war crimes, crimes against humanity and the crime of aggression. Follow full live coverage below.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur

events.title: ‘This is a fire’: DRC Ebola outbreak is fastest-growing ever, warns WHO
events.description: Infections of the Bundibugyo species of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have reached record highs and a majority of new cases are coming from “unknown chains of transmission”, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘This is a fire’: DRC Ebola outbreak is fastest-growing ever, warns WHO

events.title: Venezuela’s interim government and opposition to begin formal talks
events.description: Nobel laureate María Corina Machado will not lead negotiations over new elections, contrary to expectations The interim government of Venezuela has announced it will begin formal talks with the opposition aimed at “strengthening democracy” in the country. The move is backed by the US, which says it is seeking a “democratic transition” in a country still recovering from the twin earthquakes that killed more than 4,700 people. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuela’s interim government and opposition to begin formal talks

events.title: United by grief, mothers in Brazil demand reparations after police killed their sons
events.description: A group of Brazilian mothers whose sons were killed by police are demanding a nationwide policy to support relatives of victims of state violence and are seeking public funding to finance their activities

events.title: US launches new attacks on Iran as Tehran targets Gulf sites
events.description: Iran's Health Ministry has said at least 35 people have been killed and more than 300 injured since fighting resumed.

events.title: Ebola outbreak deepens as officials admit 'we're still chasing it'
events.description: The number of confirmed cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has reached 2,011, with 754 deaths, according to government data.

events.title: ‘Hunted’ by drones: UN reports worst month for Ukraine’s civilians since 2022
events.description: Civilian casualties in Ukraine soared during the first half of 2026 amid escalating Russian attacks and intensifying use of deadly weapons, the UN human rights monitoring mission in the country, HRMMU, said on Tuesday.

events.title: International Criminal Court ‘a critical cog’ in global justice efforts
events.description: The United Nations upheld the critical role of the International Criminal Court (ICC) on Monday in the global fight to end impunity for grave crimes, amid calls for it to be abolished.

events.title: Families of 43 victims await verdict in Genoa bridge disaster
events.description: A large section of the giant Morandi motorway bridge crumbled and collapsed on to the railway tracks below.

events.title: Dismissal of Ukraine's popular defense minister draws intense criticism
events.description: As Ukraine signs drone deals and awaits licenses for Patriot missiles, the country's popular, tech-savvy defense minister, who helped Ukraine turn the tables on Russia through drone warfare, is fired.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Dismissal of Ukraine's popular defense minister draws intense criticism

events.title: The crews stuck at sea as the Strait of Hormuz crisis grows
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz crisis is leaving thousands of crew members on commercial ships stranded. Arsenio Dominguez, head of the UN's International Maritime Organization, explains.

events.title: The EU climate policy the world copied is under fire — what's at stake?
events.description: The emissions trading system became a global blueprint for pricing carbon and cutting CO2. Now industry groups are pushing back against tougher climate rules. How effective is the scheme and where does it fall short?
events.ticker_label: 💥 The EU climate policy the world copied is under fire — what's at stake?

events.title: Venezuelans dig to find their loved ones
events.description: Two earthquakes of rare magnitude struck Venezuela on June 24, seconds apart. The state of La Guaira on the Caribbean coast was by far the hardest hit, with entire buildings collapsing there in seconds. Tens of thousands of people are still missing but help still has not arrived.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuelans dig to find their loved ones

events.title: People continue to stand by refugees, Gen Z shows most empathy
events.description: Protection for refugees has been enshrined in international law for more than seven decades, but how much support is there today for those fleeing conflict and persecution?

events.title: Greece pins hopes on heat-seeking satellites to predict wildfires
events.description: The country, which suffers each summer, builds an arsenal of high-tech surveillance to help pre-position firefighters.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Greece pins hopes on heat-seeking satellites to predict wildfires

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Top UN officials call for de-escalation in Yemen
events.description: As the UN chief’s Special Envoy for Yemen, Hans Grundberg, calls for swift de-escalation following reports of Saudi Arabian airstrikes and Iranian aircraft landing in the country, the Security Council’s emergency meeting at 3pm (local time) on Monday heard briefings from top officials on the latest humanitarian and political situation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Top UN officials call for de-escalation in Yemen

events.title: What's behind the Iran regime's 'revenge propaganda'?
events.description: Calls for revenge over the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei are growing louder in Iran. Through increasingly harsh rhetoric, the leadership is seeking to mobilize supporters at home while warning opponents abroad.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What's behind the Iran regime's 'revenge propaganda'?

events.title: French police investigate suspected pyromaniacs thought to have started Fontainebleau fire
events.description: With the two wildfires burning southeast of Paris now contained, for investigators, the focus is on how they started. Five people have been arrested, suspected of setting the blazes intentionally, including a volunteer fire fighter. Prosecutors say that a second suspect admitted to accidentally starting a fire by throwing away his cigarette.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ French police investigate suspected pyromaniacs thought to have started Fontainebleau fire

events.title: US's ICE faces fresh backlash over anti-immigration operations
events.description: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents have been ordered to pause most traffic stops in the wake of two fatal shootings. Donald Trump's so-called border tsar Tom Homan has described the move as a "temporary pause", denying it was a "policy change". Then on July 15, Trump seemingly undermined his agency, saying that Traffic Controls should continue. Meanwhile, anti-ICE protests continue in Maine, where a 25-year-old Colombian man was fatally shot two days earlier.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 US's ICE faces fresh backlash over anti-immigration operations

events.title: Death toll from Bangkok bar fire rises to 32 as 2 more die in hospital
events.description: Thai officials say that two more people injured in a huge fire at a Bangkok music bar have died, bringing the death toll to 32
events.ticker_label: 💥 Death toll from Bangkok bar fire rises to 32 as 2 more die in hospital

events.title: New guidance offers hope for reducing the global toll of dementia
events.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has issued updated global guidelines aimed at reducing the risk of cognitive decline and dementia, saying that up to 45 per cent of cases may be preventable or delayed by addressing modifiable risk factors throughout life.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz: UN agencies call for diplomacy to end attacks
events.description: As both Washington and Tehran claim to control the critical commercial shipping route through the narrow Strait of Hormuz, UN agencies on Monday are calling for de-escalation amid the recent spike in strikes in the region related to the US-Iran war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Strait of Hormuz: UN agencies call for diplomacy to end attacks

events.title: Mexico asks US state attorneys general to investigate immigrant ICE deaths
events.description: Since start of Trump’s second term, 14 Mexican immigrants have died in ICE custody and three in agency operations Mexico formally requested that US state attorneys general criminally investigate cases of immigrants who have died in Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody or during raids, the Mexican government said Tuesday. The request follows the death of Mexican immigrant Lorenzo Salgado Araujo , who was shot and killed by an ICE agent in Houston. Since the beginning of Donald Trump ’s second term, 17 Mexican immigrants have died during immigration enforcement, 14 in ICE custody and three in agency operations. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mexico asks US state attorneys general to investigate immigrant ICE deaths

events.title: Alcatraz boat capsizing in San Francisco leaves 4 dead
events.description: A three-deck pontoon vessel sank off the famous prison island, which is now a tourist destination. Three people who were missing are presumed dead after a search-and-rescue operation was called off.

events.title: Occupied West Bank school protests planned demolition by Israeli forces
events.description: Students and teachers at a primary school in the Israeli-occupied West Bank demonstrated on July 15 against its expected demolition by Israeli forces. Israel has in recent years destroyed thousands of Palestinian-owned homes & vital infrastructures, usually citing a lack of building permits. Actions which have repeatedly been condemned by human rights organisations and the international community.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Occupied West Bank school protests planned demolition by Israeli forces

events.title: Baltics and Poland warn Russia could launch limited military or hybrid provocation against NATO
events.description: Lithuania's president says intelligence suggests Russia is planning attacks on critical infrastructure in the Baltic states or Poland
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Baltics and Poland warn Russia could launch limited military or hybrid provocation against NATO

events.title: West Africa and the Sahel: Terrorism is changing its face
events.description: Jihadist groups are no longer content with launching attacks in West Africa and the Sahel: they administer territories, control trade routes, exploit new technologies and are gradually pushing their influence all the way to the Gulf of Guinea.
events.ticker_label: 💥 West Africa and the Sahel: Terrorism is changing its face

events.title: Inside Israel's mission to train civilians to stop the next Oct 7-like terror attack
events.description: A full-scale terror attack drill at Kibbutz Bror Hayil simulates Oct. 7-style infiltration, training civil defense squads near the Gaza border.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Inside Israel's mission to train civilians to stop the next Oct 7-like terror attack

events.title: Russian attacks kill 14 as Ukraine hits Black Sea oil tankers
events.description: Civilian casualties are reported in a number of Ukrainian regions, while Kyiv hits 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea.

events.title: FIFA World Cup final: Date, time, halftime show, prediction and Trump
events.description: From Donald Trump's attendance to the match winner, the short guide before football’s global showpiece in New Jersey.

events.title: Police release images of clothes to help identify victims of alleged Sydney childcare paedophile
events.description: AFP release images of children’s clothes found on electronic devices seized as part of investigation into Hamish Tait Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A tiny dinosaur puffer jacket could lead police to more victims of a former childcare worker and alleged prolific child sex offender. Federal police have released pictures of clothing from children they want to identify, urging parents and carers who recognise the items to come forward. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Police release images of clothes to help identify victims of alleged Sydney childcare paedophile

events.title: Fire at orphanage in Algeria kills 11 and wounds 19
events.description: A fire at an orphanage outside of Algiers claimed the lives of at least 11 people and wounded 19, the country's civil defence announced on Thursday.

events.title: Ukraine approves Sergiy Koretsky as new PM amid protests over cabinet reshuffle
events.description: Ukraine's parliament ​voted on Thursday ​to appoint energy executive Sergiy Koretsky as ​prime minister, ‌the ⁠third since the ‌start of Russia's invasion ⁠in 2022. The latest cabinet reshuffle sparked rare wartime protests across the country, with demonstrators demanding that President Volodymyr Zelensky reappoint the popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, who was removed from office on Wednesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine approves Sergiy Koretsky as new PM amid protests over cabinet reshuffle

events.title: Childhood vaccination rate increases slightly, but millions remain unprotected
events.description: Global childhood immunization programmes continued to recover in 2025, but conflict, poverty and growing vaccine hesitancy are still leaving millions vulnerable to preventable diseases, according to new UN data released on Wednesday.

events.title: Fire at orphanage in Algeria kills 11 people, including children
events.description: Eleven people died and 19 were injured after a fire swept through a state-run child welfare home in east Algiers.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Fire at orphanage in Algeria kills 11 people, including children

events.title: Brazil condemns US move to impose 25% tariffs next week
events.description: Trade office cites investigation of Brazilian trade practices while Marco Rubio says Lula’s policies ‘bad for Americans and bad for Brazilians’ Brazil has condemned the US decision to impose 25% tariffs on certain Brazilian products, after the Trump administration found a range of what it deemed unfair trade practices by the South American country. “The Brazilian government repudiates the decision announced today by the United States government regarding the imposition of 25 percent tariffs on Brazilian products,” the office of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said in a statement on X, denying it had engaged in unfair trade practices. Continue reading...

events.title: Australian patients’ medical records could be sold on dark web after clinics’ data breach
events.description: ‘Malicious actor’ obtains sensitive data including Medicare numbers, treatment details and pathology results in cyber-attack on Partnered Health Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australians’ medical records and patient information could be sold on the hidden market, an expert has warned, after a cyber-attack at one of the nation’s biggest healthcare providers. Partnered Health revealed 21 clinics across several cities including Sydney, Melbourne and Canberra were affected when a “malicious actor” accessed its data on 23 June. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australian patients’ medical records could be sold on dark web after clinics’ data breach

events.title: Senegal's 'plastic man' helps raise awareness about plastic pollution
events.description: In West Africa, plastic waste is a major environmental challenge. In Senegal, activist Modou Fall has taken an unusual approach to raising awareness about plastic pollution. He walks through Dakar and other parts of the country wearing an eye-catching outfit made from discarded plastic waste. His goal is to highlight the scale of the problem and urge authorities to enforce environmental protection laws.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Senegal's 'plastic man' helps raise awareness about plastic pollution

events.title: Seattle fire crews respond to 3-alarm Industrial District warehouse fire, roof partially collapses
events.description: Two tractor trailers caught fire and spread to a Seattle warehouse, causing a partial roof collapse as firefighters battled the 3-alarm blaze.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Seattle fire crews respond to 3-alarm Industrial District warehouse fire, roof partially collapses

events.title: Trump won’t rule out Kharg Island takeover: What a US assault could look like
events.description: Military experts detail how U.S. Marines could seize Iran's Kharg Island in hours but warn holding it against missile and drone attacks is far riskier.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump won’t rule out Kharg Island takeover: What a US assault could look like

events.title: Argentina face action over Falklands banner
events.description: Argentina face the prospect of disciplinary action from Fifa after their players celebrate the World Cup semi-final win against England with a banner in support of their country's claims to the Falkland Islands.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Could Russia be about to lose its grip on chess leadership?
events.description: Russians have long pulled the strings when it comes to the governing body of international chess. But now two Germans are vying for FIDE's top job.

events.title: How India's crime gangs went global
events.description: The FBI's largest crackdown on India-linked organized crime shows how the Lawrence Bishnoi network evolved into a transnational law-enforcement challenge.

events.title: Ukrainians protest the dismissal of Kyiv's 'most successful defence minister'
events.description: Russian missiles targeted the Ukrainian capital early on July 16, but it hasn't deterred protesters who are out in the streets opposing the dismissal of the popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov. During his tenure, Federov oversaw a series of successful Ukrainian operations, including strikes on energy targets deep inside Russia.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukrainians protest the dismissal of Kyiv's 'most successful defence minister'

events.title: Ukrainian prime minister resigns in Zelenskyy shake-up
events.description: Yulia Svyrydenko resigned as Ukraine's prime minister, with Zelenskyy saying he offered her a new role in relations with a key international partner.

events.title: UK calls for FIFA investigation over Argentina banner
events.description: Following their 2-1 World Cup semifinal victory over England, Argentine players held up a banner claiming the Falkland Islands belong to Argentina. Political messages are banned under FIFA rules.

events.title: South Korea's central bank hikes rate for 1st time since 2023 to curb inflation, debt
events.description: South Korea’s central bank on Thursday raised its key interest rate for the first time in more than three years, aiming to tighten money supply to combat inflation, worsened by the war in the Middle East, and slow the growth of the country’s high house...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 South Korea's central bank hikes rate for 1st time since 2023 to curb inflation, debt

events.title: UK pins string of antisemitic attacks on Iran-linked group, bans IRGC
events.description: Keir Starmer announced sweeping national security powers after British intelligence linked antisemitic arson attacks to an Iran-backed proxy.

events.title: Four Americans caught in horrific Mexico highway pileup that killed at least 10
events.description: A fiery chain-reaction crash involving multiple tractor-trailers killed at least 10 people in western Mexico, while four Americans were transported to local hospitals.

events.title: Eleven people die in fire at Algerian children's care home
events.description: Along with the fatalities, 19 people suffered injuries, the authorities say.

events.title: US strikes Iran, tanker, as Tehran hits Kuwait, Jordan: What’s the latest
events.description: Strikes have hit closer to Tehran for the first time since the latest wave of attacks started.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US strikes Iran, tanker, as Tehran hits Kuwait, Jordan: What’s the latest

events.title: Assistant multicultural affairs minister ‘appalled’ by Tony Abbott’s anti-immigration video – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Liberals say ‘all options’ should be considered despite Angus Taylor hosing down gender quota proposal Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Michelle Rowland says mandatory minimum standards for datacentres don’t apply to existing developments The attorney general, Michelle Rowland , told ABC News that mandatory minimum standards for datacentres would apply to new developments, not existing ones. The scope and the timing of those is something that I think will be properly determined by national cabinet. This is an issue that not only state governments but local councils have been dealing with as well. It goes from being this hunched-over ball of fluff at the top of a tree looking down at you, to suddenly spreading out its limbs and flying over your head like a magical flying carpet, or this weird stingray-UFO thing in the sky. Continue reading...

events.title: Snake in a chain: lucky escape for Australian woman after bite from deadly eastern brown caught in bike wheel
events.description: Woman in her 60s was riding on popular cycling trail in northern NSW when she ran over the two-metre-long eastern brown snake, one of the world’s most venomous A woman in her 60s is recovering after being bitten by a two-metre-long eastern brown snake that had become entangled in her bike chain in regional Australia. She was riding on the northern rivers rail trail near Burringbar, in the Tweed shire in northern New South Wales, when she ran over the snake. Continue reading...

events.title: Ukraine lawmakers approves Koretskyi as new PM amid protests
events.description: It is President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's second Cabinet shakeup in a year. He faces criticism for dismissing reformist Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Follow DW for more.

events.title: Ahead of French PM's visit, Rabat faces allegations of spying on Paris
events.description: French Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu is set to visit Morocco on July 16 in an effort to strengthen ties between Paris and Rabat, whether it is for trade or defence. But as the visit starts, an investigation has revealed that Morocco has used Israeli spyware to spy on several French ministers, including the French prime minister himself. FRANCE 24's Shirli Sitbon tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ahead of French PM's visit, Rabat faces allegations of spying on Paris

events.title: Eleven killed in fire at Algerian orphanage
events.description: Eleven people, including children, have been killed in a fire at an Algerian orphanage, according to authorities.

events.title: Independent expert urges UK leaders to uphold Supreme Court ruling on women’s rights
events.description: A UN independent human rights expert has urged political leaders in the United Kingdom to support implementation of a Supreme Court ruling on the legal definition of sex, warning that opposition to updated equality guidance risks weakening protections for women and girls.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Independent expert urges UK leaders to uphold Supreme Court ruling on women’s rights

events.title: WATCH: Russian soldier thrown through air as Soviet-era helicopter gun spins out of control
events.description: Trump says Putin is "ready to make a deal" to end the Ukraine war, but Russia warned Western troops enforcing any ceasefire would be military targets.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WATCH: Russian soldier thrown through air as Soviet-era helicopter gun spins out of control

events.title: A new chapter for America’s 250th: 'J250' initiative puts Jewish contributions in the spotlight
events.description: The J250 Initiative launches with 250 stories of Jewish American patriots, scientists and leaders who shaped the nation from the Revolution onward.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A new chapter for America’s 250th: 'J250' initiative puts Jewish contributions in the spotlight

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Moroccan intelligence insider reveals widespread use of Pegasus hacking software
events.description: Whistleblower suggests internal security services deployed spyware from 2017 against key domestic and foreign targets A former member of Morocco’s domestic intelligence service has helped to provide an unprecedented insight into how the north African state used hacking software – including Pegasus spyware – to target journalists, human rights defenders, French politicians and Spanish cabinet ministers and police officers. Pegasus, which is manufactured by the Israel-based NSO Group, allows its operator to access everything on a target’s mobile phone, including emails, text messages and photographs. It can also activate the phone’s recorder and camera, turning it into a listening device. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Moroccan intelligence insider reveals widespread use of Pegasus hacking software

events.title: East-West border death zone now Germany's biodiversity oasis
events.description: Many endangered species now live in the area that once formed the border between East and West Germany. Environment Minister Carsten Schneider recently visited the "Green Belt," and DW went along for the ride.

events.title: World News in Brief: Schools closed in West Bank, AI in healthcare, Indigenous rights
events.description: Access to education continues to worsen in the West Bank, the United Nations reported on Wednesday.

events.title: US troops unload China's only known aid flight to earthquake-ravaged Venezuela as Americans surge relief
events.description: U.S. government photos show American soldiers unloading China's only aid flight at Simón Bolívar Airport as Venezuela reels from June earthquakes.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ US troops unload China's only known aid flight to earthquake-ravaged Venezuela as Americans surge relief

events.title: Zelenskyy fires Ukraine's tech-savvy defense minister in government reshuffle
events.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired the country's popular defense minister, who pushed for innovation in the battlefield through the use of drones and turned the tables on Russia.

events.title: The political risks from war in Iran. And, ICE's use of force is rising, report finds
events.description: The political implications the war in Iran is having on the GOP. And, a new report has found that ICE officers' use of force is not rare since President Trump took office for his second term.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The political risks from war in Iran. And, ICE's use of force is rising, report finds

events.title: The band playing when a Bangkok bar caught fire mourns its members among the 33 dead
events.description: Victims of this week's flash fire at a Bangkok music bar that took more than 30 lives included four of the six core members of the band playing when the blaze broke out.

events.title: India news: Murders following romantic rejections increase
events.description: The stabbing of a Bengaluru student after she reportedly turned down a marriage proposal is among a string of recent murders across India that involve violence following romantic rejections. DW has more.

events.title: Drunk wrong-way driver killed Mass trooper after 9 drinks at bar, DA report says
events.description: Hernan Marrero was served nine drinks at Tribu Mexican Kitchen and Bar in Saugus before driving the wrong way on Route 1 and killing Trooper Kevin Trainor.

events.title: More people around the world now favour China over the US, Pew study suggests
events.description: There is more confidence in Xi Jinping than Donald Trump, the US think tank's survey indicates.

events.title: Photos: Messi’s Argentina stun England to reach World Cup final vs Spain
events.description: Messi inspires Argentina’s dramatic late comeback win over England to reach final against Spain.

events.title: Watch FIFA World Cup: Argentina rallies past England to reach final
events.description: Latest match highlights, reaction and previews from the FIFA World Cup 2026 in North America.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: No Asian team managed to go far this World Cup (again). What's holding them back?
events.description: For the sixth World Cup in a row, no team from the Asian Football Confederation made it past the first knockout round, winning only three games out of 29. Asian teams are competitive — up to a point.

events.title: A week of strikes shatters hope of a lasting US-Iran peace deal
events.description: Hopes of a lasting peace deal between Iran and the US are fading fast following a week that has seen consistent exchange of fire between the two sides. The US resumed its blockade on the Strait of Hormuz firing at a ship that attempted to breach it. For its part, Iran retaliated by launching strikes on US allies including Jordan, where projectiles believed to be of Iranian origin were spotted.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A week of strikes shatters hope of a lasting US-Iran peace deal

events.title: 'If you don't suffer, it's not Argentina': fans react to dramatic semifinal
events.description: Argentina have secured their spot in the World Cup final, after defeating England 2-1. It had been England who initially took the lead, with Anthony Gordon scoring in the 55th minute. However, the remainder of the match saw a reversal of fortune, as Lautaro Martinez and Enzo Fernandez both netted late in the match. Argentina will face Spain in the final on July 19.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Huge crowds in Argentina celebrate victory over England
events.description: Hundreds of thousands of Argentina fans packed the streets of Buenos Aires on Wednesday night to celebrate victory over England and a place in the World Cup final. Defending champions Argentina scored two late goals to come from behind and win 2-1. They will now meet Spain in Sunday’s final.

events.title: World Cup Rewind: Argentina rejoices as it reaches second final in a row
events.description: Argentina will play Spain in the World Cup final on July 19 after Lionel Messi orchestrated a spectacular comeback against England in a semi-final with a dramatic ending. The defeat was another bitter blow for the Three Lions, who have reached at least the semi-final stage in four of their past five major tournaments without getting over the line. FRANCE 24's James Vasina tells us more about what to know.

events.title: India's Gen Z 'cockroaches' took protest to the streets
events.description: An activist's hunger strike has become a rallying point for India’s new Cockroach Janta Party, a youth-led movement that emerged in response to exam paper leaks and student suicides
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India's Gen Z 'cockroaches' took protest to the streets

events.title: World Cup football’s other goal: Helping young people talk about mental health
events.description: When Dr. Sahira Al Nahari founded Shifā Art, a Saudi Arabia-based organization using art to create conversations around mental health, she noticed that men attending her therapeutic art workshops often felt uncomfortable opening up about their feelings.

events.title: World News in Brief: Gaza aid challenges persist, renewed push for clean energy, Sudan cholera update
events.description: Humanitarians in the Gaza Strip continue to face significant challenges in reaching communities located near the so-called “Yellow Line” in northern Rafah, the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Tuesday.

events.title: Three teen girls arrested in New Jersey for allegedly throwing acid on six women in drive-by attack
events.description: Three teen girls on a moped allegedly threw sulfuric acid at six women in Jersey City, causing skin burns and sending one victim to a burn unit.

events.title: Actor Sam Neill died from pneumonia, agent confirms
events.description: Neill died in Sydney on Monday, with his family saying his death was 'sudden and unexpected'.

events.title: ‘Safe passage’: The fatal ambiguity at the heart of the Hormuz MoU
events.description: The vague language of the US-Iran MoU made the present escalation in the Strait of Hormuz all but inevitable.

events.title: Decades of denial: Death certificate for disappeared reopens Kashmir wounds
events.description: After years of searching and court battles, a judge declares what Junaid Rashid already believed: His father was dead.

events.title: Why Italy’s Genoa bridge collapse led to one of its biggest criminal trials
events.description: Eight years after 43 people were killed, judges rule in the first trial over the Genoa bridge collapse.

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: New US Ebola patient arrives in Germany for treatment
events.description: Aid worker flown to Berlin as Trump administration bars Americans from traveling to US on commercial flights A US national who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has arrived in Germany for treatment, the health ministry in Berlin said on Monday, weeks after another American infected with Ebola in the DRC was treated in Berlin. Meanwhile, the Trump administration on Monday said it was blocking American citizens in ⁠the DRC from traveling to the US on commercial flights, Reuters reported, citing a White House official. Continue reading...

events.title: Fears for New Zealand’s native species as first bird flu case emerges
events.description: Minister urges public to report cases of three or more sick or dead birds in a group after brown skua seabird tests positive for H5N1 on Wellington beach The deadly H5N1 bird flu has been confirmed in New Zealand for the first time, sparking alarm that some of the country’s most beloved and vulnerable native birds could be wiped out if it spreads. A single ocean-going seabird, a brown skua, returned a confirmed positive test on Wednesday, after it was found on Petone beach in Wellington on 10 July, said Andrew Hoggard, the biosecurity minister. Continue reading...

events.title: Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?
events.description: The U.S. has fought three major wars in the past quarter-century, in Iran, Iraq and Afghanistan. None has gone as planned.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?

events.title: US imposes a 25 percent tariff on some Brazilian imports, citing unfair trade practices
events.description: The United States will slap 25 percent tariffs on some Brazilian goods starting July 22 following a yearlong investigation under the Trade Act of 1974. The probe concluded that Brazil engaged in unfair trade practices. Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva condemned the move, saying it was politically motivated to favour his rival for upcoming elections, Flavio Bolsonaro. Also in this edition, we look at the Japanese firm trying to bring back 1980's style corporate culture.

events.title: Twin attacks by militants kill 3 police officers, wound 20 in restive northwest Pakistan
events.description: Officials say a suicide bomber and a group of militants have attacked a police station and a security convoy hour apart in restive northwest Pakistan, killing at least three police officers and wounding 20 others
events.ticker_label: 💥 Twin attacks by militants kill 3 police officers, wound 20 in restive northwest Pakistan

events.title: Lebanon and Israel move toward implementing withdrawal agreement, US officials say

events.title: Colombia: Pockets of insecurity challenge ‘vibrant democracy’, Security Council told
events.description: As Colombia awaits the inauguration of its newly elected president, pockets of insecurity remain a challenge for a nation that has overcome decades of conflict, the head of the UN verification team there told the Security Council on Wednesday.

events.title: Looted gold and gum arabic are bankrolling Sudan's war, UN warns

events.title: Singapore court orders Bloomberg to pay ministers $356,000 in defamation case
events.description: Bloomberg’s editor-in-chief, John Micklethwait, stands by reporting, saying ministers who sued ‘imposed an extremely strained meaning on what was a solid ​story’ Bloomberg News and one of its reporters have been ordered to pay S$460,000 (US$355,734) in damages after an article it published was found to have defamed two Singapore government ministers, the city-state’s high court said in a judgment released on Tuesday. Bloomberg and the reporter, Low De Wei, are liable to jointly pay S$230,000 to each minister, comprising S$170,000 in general damages and S$60,000 in aggravated damages, ⁠the judgment said. Continue reading...

events.title: Irish police under fire for refusing to identify reported asylum seeker sought in American mother's murder

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: AI: Why Europe is falling behind, and how it can catch up
events.description: Europe trails the US and China in artificial intelligence. Economist and Nobel laureate Philippe Aghion says better research funding, venture capital and AI innovation can help it catch up.

events.title: China’s economy grows at 4.3%, one of its lowest rates on record
events.description: Worse-than-expected figures for three months to June come amid concerns over lopsided economy Business live – latest updates China has posted worse-than-expected growth figures for the three months to June as its economy expanded by just 4.3% – one of its lowest quarterly readings on record. The rate, which came in under the government’s target of 4.5% to 5%, was one of the weakest since reporting on official quarterly GDP figures began in the early 1990s. Continue reading...

events.title: As the smoke clears, the Fontainebleau forest takes stock of its destruction
events.description: Approaching 2,000 hectares of the iconic and fragile Fontainebleau forest have burned out with around 950 firefighters supported by aerial teams still continuing to battle the flames. While the fire has been contained, it has yet to be extinguished four days after it broke out. French President Emmanuel Macron is due to visit the site.

events.title: 🔴 Ukraine approves Sergiy Koretsky as new PM amid protests over cabinet reshuffle
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Protests erupt in Ukraine as Zelensky removes 'his most popular' minister
events.description: Large protests erupted in several Ukrainian cities on July 16 against the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov by President Volodymyr Zelensky. Only appointed six months ago, Fedorov carved a reputation as a moderniser who tried to reform the Ukrainian military, worn down by more than four years of the Russian invasion. FRANCE 24's Emmanuelle Chaze tells us more from one of the protests.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Protests erupt in Ukraine as Zelensky removes 'his most popular' minister

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Japan officials hunt bear that raided couple’s fridge amid string of break-ins
events.description: Officials set up traps and electric fences after 14 break-ins recored in one town in two weeks, amid fears of a repeat offender Authorities are searching for a bear that sneaked into the home of an elderly couple and raided their fridge amid concerns it may be behind 14 break-ins across a Japanese town in the past fortnight. On Monday evening, Mitsuo Matsubara, 87, was confronted by a large Asiatic black bear when he went to investigate a noise in his kitchen. His fridge was open , and food was strewn across the floor. His wife called the police. Continue reading...

events.title: China and Xi favored over U.S. and Trump in many nations: Survey

events.title: Zambia's Guy Scott, who was Africa's first white head of state in decades, has died at 82

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Liberals say ‘all options’ should be considered despite Angus Taylor hosing down gender quota proposal
events.description: Anne Ruston called for the party to act with ‘urgency’ after discussion paper suggested ways to improve engagement with women, multicultural communities and young people Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Angus Taylor has hosed down a proposal for the Liberal party to consider introducing gender quotas, while one of his senior shadow cabinet ministers has implored the party to act on such ideas with “urgency”. After a discussion paper that canvassed the idea was circulated to supporters on Wednesday, the party’s leader said he has never been supportive of quotas – shortly before his deputy, Jane Hume, intervened to say all options would be considered. Continue reading...

events.title: Myanmar: More than 500 feared dead in capsized refugee boats, UN says
events.description: The UN has reported that two vessels carrying Rohingya refugees set off in hazardous conditions and are believed to have capsized. In recent years, thousands of Rohingya have attempted dangerous sea crossings.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million

events.description: Hong Kong was once known for its freedom of publication, but political changes have created a challenging environment for independent bookstores.

events.title: US slaps 25% tariffs on Brazil with election looming
events.description: The US will impose a 25% tariff on most imports from Brazil starting later this month. It's the first action in the Trump administration's new tariff strategy, after the Supreme Court struck down its prior impositions.

events.title: Germany news: Gender switch neo-Nazi sent to men's prison
events.description: Neo-Nazi Marla Svenja Liebich has been transferred to a men's prison after extradition back to Germany, despite being legally registered as a woman. DW has more.

events.title: Will gold prices extend their record-breaking run?
events.description: Predictions suggest the price of the precious metal will keep hitting new record highs as investors seek a safe bet in turbulent times. Some forecasts say prices could double within five years.

events.title: Hong Kong police raid two bookstores, arrest five people
events.description: Five booksellers have been arrested in Hong Kong over the sale of allegedly "seditious" publications. Two bookstores have become the latest target in a continued crackdown on independent bookshops

events.title: Trump administration replaces Philadelphia slavery memorial
events.description: The Trump administration has replaced panels at a Philadelphia memorial about people enslaved by George Washington. Critics argue that the new exhibit presents a sanitized view of US history.

events.title: France's parliament passes assisted dying law
events.description: The lower house of France's parliament adopted bill for assisted dying. But the the government bypassed the right-wing dominated Senate and the law will instead go to the highest constitutional court for final approval.

events.title: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz makes appeal to AfD voters
events.description: Germany's chancellor has insisted that his coalition has "regained its footing" — despite dismal poll numbers. Friedrich Merz also cautioned against voting for the far-right AFD party.
events.ticker_label: 💥 German Chancellor Friedrich Merz makes appeal to AfD voters

events.title: Kenya: Court rejects Rastafari bid for religious freedom marijuana exemption
events.description: Kenya's small Rastafarian community was seeking permission to smoke marijuana under the constitutional right to freedom of religion. The judge found that they did not show it to be a necessary part of their worship.

events.title: Jurassic Park star died from pneumonia, agent says
events.description: Jurassic Park and Peaky Blinders star Sam Neill died from pneumonia after beating cancer, his agent said.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Jurassic Park star died from pneumonia, agent says

events.title: Gus the T-rex breaks dinosaur fossil sale record at auction
events.description: A Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton has become the most expensive dinosaur fossil sold at auction after an unidentified buyer paid more than $50m (£37.3m) for it.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Gus the T-rex breaks dinosaur fossil sale record at auction

events.title: UK working to ban goods and services exports to and imports from Israeli settlements
events.description: The UK government is working on a ban on imports and exports to illegal Israeli settlements, the trade minister confirmed on Wednesday morning.

events.title: A mass trial of MS-13 gang members in El Salvador wraps up in El Salvador
events.description: A mass trial of hundreds of alleged MS-13 gang members has wrapped up in El Salvador with prosecutors delivering their closing arguments

events.title: Jordan's military says it shot down 8 missiles launched by Iran targeting the kingdom
events.description: Jordan's military says it shot down 8 missiles launched by Iran targeting the kingdom
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Jordan's military says it shot down 8 missiles launched by Iran targeting the kingdom

events.title: Ukraine buries its unknown soldiers as families wait for identification
events.description: In Kyiv, a brother and sister walk through a military cemetery, carrying carnations

events.title: Former Hungarian foreign minister resigns from parliament for job with Chinese automaker
events.description: Hungary's former foreign minister, Péter Szijjártó, has resigned from parliament to join Chinese electric automaker BYD

events.title: Skills for an uncertain future: How youth can navigate a changing job market
events.description: Choosing a path for the future has never been easy, but for young people today, such rapid technological change as artificial intelligence (AI) is making it increasingly difficult to predict which skills will remain relevant in the years ahead.

events.title: Trump teases major reveal during White House address and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Two families sue North Dakota hospital on allegations babies were switched at birth
events.description: Two men say a North Dakota hospital switched them at birth, a mix-up discovered through DNA testing more than 30 years later, according to a lawsuit.

events.title: ICE arrests record 238 illegal immigrants in one day during South Texas enforcement operation
events.description: ICE's Rio Grande Valley field office in Harlingen arrested 238 illegal immigrants in a single day, including convicted gang members and violent offenders.

events.title: Retired math professor charged after wife, an airline meteorologist, found shot dead: cops
events.description: Leland Jameson, a retired George Mason University math professor, allegedly shot his wife Chisen Jameson, an All Nippon Airways meteorologist.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Retired math professor charged after wife, an airline meteorologist, found shot dead: cops

events.title: Nevada governor name-drops during traffic stop and avoids red-light citation
events.description: Body camera video shows Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo identifying himself during a Las Vegas traffic stop before driving away without a citation.

events.title: Florida school principals face DUI charges after police traffic stops just hours apart
events.description: Body camera video shows two Clermont, Florida principals arrested on DUI charges in separate traffic stops involving the same white Jeep SUV.

events.title: Slain American mother Jamey Carney remembered as 'ray of sunshine' at Ireland funeral
events.description: Jamey Carney's livestreamed funeral in Killarney celebrated her life as a "ray of sunshine" while the Jordanian suspect remains detained abroad.

events.title: Weightlifter accused of collecting $100K in disability while showing off in the gym online
events.description: Raymond Phillips allegedly posted weightlifting videos on social media while collecting over $100,000 in Social Security disability benefits.

events.title: Police say Canadian woman slapped teen over Trump and ICE clothing before ICE detained her
events.description: Surveillance video captured Kaitlyn Tracey allegedly slapping a teen over Trump and ICE pants on the Point Pleasant Beach boardwalk before her arrest.

events.title: Trump AG pick Todd Blanche faces Senate test and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: African growth boom follows Trump push to replace aid with trade
events.description: Despite predictions of collapse after USAID cuts, sub-Saharan Africa is outpacing Asia in growth forecasts, fueled by trade and private investment.

events.title: Who is Andy Burnham? The Trump critic set to become the UK's next prime minister
events.description: Andy Burnham secured 322 MP nominations on day one of the leadership process, but critics say he faces less scrutiny than any recent U.K. prime minister.

events.title: Trump officials unveil private sector blueprint for life after USAID
events.description: Ambassador Mike Waltz says the Trump administration is replacing traditional foreign aid with private investment and trade to create jobs, cut dependency.

events.title: FBI snares an American heir indicted for allegedly bankrolling anti-cop, pro-Hamas communist revolution
events.description: James Cox Chambers Jr., estranged heir to the $27 billion Cox empire, was detained in Spain for alleged money laundering and Hamas support.
